subject: Mathematics
title: Hilbert's 23 Problems
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Hilbert's 23 Problems.

hook.text: In 1900, David Hilbert stood before the world's mathematicians and issued a challenge.

buildup.text: He listed 23 unsolved problems that he believed would guide the next century of math.

discovery.text: Problems ranged from the Riemann Hypothesis to the foundations of geometry.

twist.text: Some were solved quickly. Others remain unsolved 125 years later.

climax.text: His list shaped the entire trajectory of 20th-century mathematics.

punchline.text: One speech at a conference steered a century of discovery.

quiz.question: What did Hilbert present in 1900?
options: A list of 23 unsolved mathematical problems
A proof of Fermat's Last Theorem
